title: how to set podman container cgroup parent directory?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

1.  create cgroup in "/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/test111/ccc ":

2. start a container with --cgroup-parent=<path/to/cgroupdir>:
[leo@centos8 ~]$ podman run  --cgroup-parent=/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/test111/ccc -v /etc/group:/etc/group -v /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd --userns=keep-id -it -w `pwd` -v `pwd`:`pwd` -v /tmp:/tmp --rm centos /bin/bash
[leo@49cc2d558f95 ~]$ id
uid=1000(leo) gid=1000(leo) groups=1000(leo)
[leo@49cc2d558f95 ~]$ ls -l /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/test111/ccc


3. check cgroup-parent:
[leo@centos8 ccc]$ pwd
/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/test111/ccc
[leo@centos8 ccc]$ ll
total 0
-rw-r--r-- 1 leo leo 0 May 29 14:50 cgroup.clone_children
-rw-r--r-- 1 leo leo 0 May 29 14:50 cgroup.procs

-rw-r--r-- 1 leo leo 0 May 29 14:50 notify_on_release
-rw-r--r-- 1 leo leo 0 May 29 14:50 tasks
[leo@centos8 ccc]$ cat cgroup.procs
[leo@centos8 ccc]$ docker ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND    CREATED        STATUS            PORTS  NAMES
49cc2d558f95  docker.io/library/centos:latest  /bin/bash  4 minutes ago  Up 4 minutes ago         inspiring_hermann

nothing is created in the cgroup-parent directory.

In docker itself, a directory named <container_id> should be created in cgroup-parent directory.

seems podman --cgroup-parent does not work.

or I did not use the parameter correctly ?


**Describe the results you received:**

comments.body:
First: this is not expected to work. Podman does not use cgroups on cgroups v1 systems in rootless mode (there are security issues with cgroups v1 that make them unsafe for use without root access - v2, as used in Fedora 31/32, resolve these issues). We will not create cgroups as rootless on a cgroups v1 system, as your `podman info` output indicates your host is. It might be a good idea for us to disable the `--cgroup-parent` flag in this case, but for now it is a no-op.

If you do decide to do this as root: don't specify the full directory - specify the portion after `/sys/fs/cgroup` (so `-cgroup-parent=/test111/ccc`). Also, make sure you are using the `cgroupfs` cgroup manager, not `systemd` - the `systemd` driver expects to be given a slice to run under.

Also, be aware that you only created a cgroup for a single subsystem there (CPU). Podman uses a lot more controllers than just that, so it will create that cgroup for all of the other controllers where it may be missing (e.g. memory)

@mheon
Got it, thank you for your answer :)
I think this is answered, so closing, feel free to reopen if there are further questions
